title: As Trump nukes science, is India's brain drain ready for 'brain regain'?
content: The US President's dismissal of the National Science Board and other scientific bodies has sparked concerns about the erosion of scientific norms. This move, alongside funding cuts and grant cancellations, is prompting foreign-origin scientists to consider leaving the US, with India actively recruiting talent.

title: ‘Today was my day’: Shubman Gill reveals ‘lucky break’ in GT win over RCB
content: Shubman Gill's aggressive 43 off 18 balls, aided by early luck, propelled Gujarat Titans to a four-wicket victory over Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Gill's powerplay blitz set the tone, despite a middle-order wobble, with Rahul Tewatia providing crucial late runs. RCB skipper Rajat Patidar acknowledged Gill's decisive impact, while praising his team's bowling effort in stretching the chase.

title: KPMG to lay off hundreds of employees in its advisory division in America
content: KPMG has initiated layoffs impacting around 400 employees in its US Advisory division, a move stemming from a strategic restructuring due to shifting demand in consulting services. While regulatory risk, customer operations, and financial services are affected, growth areas like AI and strategy remain strong. This follows earlier workforce adjustments in the audit business.

title: Structured tender model for shipbuilding: Govt aims tech transfer, local manufacturing
content: India is boosting its shipbuilding capabilities by adopting a "structured tender" approach for specialized vessels like gas and oil tankers. This strategy mandates that a portion of these ships be built domestically, facilitating technology transfer and strengthening local manufacturing. The government aims to aggregate demand for hundreds of vessels to enhance self-reliance in the maritime sector.

title: US cutting firepower near Iran? Supercarrier USS Gerald R Ford set to exit Middle East
content: The USS Gerald R. Ford carrier strike group is heading home from the Middle East after an extended 10-month deployment, reducing US naval power amid ongoing Iran tensions. This withdrawal, despite stalled diplomatic efforts and continued blockade enforcement, marks the longest deployment for a modern US carrier, raising concerns about crew and vessel readiness.

title: Justice dept sues $5B tech firm for excluding Americans from applying to high-paying jobs
content: The US Department of Justice is suing tech firm Cloudera for allegedly discriminating against American job seekers. Prosecutors claim the company created a sham hiring process, favoring temporary visa holders for lucrative roles and actively deterring US workers. This lawsuit highlights the DOJ's commitment to protecting domestic employment opportunities.

title: Horoscope today: Daily astrological predictions for May 1
content: Horoscope Today: Read daily horoscope predictions for May 1, 2026. Know all about the astrological events and influences that will be affecting each of the 12 zodiac signs. Our astrologer has analyzed the movements of the planets and the alignment of the stars to bring you the most accurate and up-to-date horoscope predictions for the day ahead. Get ready to discover what the stars have in store for you today.

title: Shubman Gill’s 43 off 18 powers GT to dominant win over RCB with 25 balls to spare
content: Gujarat Titans secured a commanding six-wicket victory over Royal Challengers Bengaluru, successfully chasing 156. Skipper Shubman Gill's aggressive 43 off 18 balls set the tone, with Jos Buttler and Rahul Tewatia providing crucial support. Earlier, RCB faltered after a strong start, collapsing to 155 due to excellent bowling from Arshad Khan and Jason Holder.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO collection: Ranveer's film races towards 'Baahubali 2' global mark
content: In a remarkable cinematic journey, Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2' is swiftly approaching the monumental box office figures of 'Baahubali 2,' securing its spot as the top-grossing film of the year. After spending six weeks captivating audiences worldwide, it has amassed a staggering Rs 1,782.15 crore, now ranking as the third highest-grossing Indian film of all time. Read on to know more in detail.

title: After two months, Google withdraws from Pentagon's $100m drone contest
content: Google quietly withdrew from a Pentagon competition to build voice-controlled autonomous drone swarms—after initially being selected as a successful submission. The $100 million contest, run by Special Operations Command and the Defense Innovation Unit, seeks technology to direct drone swarms in real-time battlefield scenarios. Google cited "resourcing" issues, but the exit is said to be  followed by an internal ethics review. OpenAI, Palantir, and xAI remain in the running.

title: Monthly domestic urea output back to last year’s levels
content: Domestic urea production has bounced back to last year's levels, with April output reaching 21 lakh tonnes, a significant recovery after a March dip.  With natural gas supplies largely restored and robust import assurances, the government confirms ample fertilizer availability to meet the upcoming kharif crop needs, ensuring farmers remain unaffected by global price hikes.

title: Archana, Farah back Rahul Roy amid trolling over viral reels
content: Actor Rahul Roy is facing online criticism for his viral reels, but many are rallying in support. Roy revealed financial struggles due to legal matters, prompting encouragement from celebrities like Farah Khan and Archana Puran Singh. He defended his work, emphasizing honesty and seeking genuine opportunities over mockery.

title: Today's Chanakya exit poll: BJP to sweep Bengal in setback for Mamata; TVK big disruptor in TN
content: Today's Chanakya exit polls predict a landslide victory for the BJP in Assam, indicating a second term for Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma. In Kerala, the projections show a close contest between the incumbent LDF and the UDF, with the BJP expected to secure a few seats. Projections for West Bengal and Tamil Nadu are still awaited.

title: Jason Whitlock links Russini to NFL coaches in bizarre claim
content: Former FOX Sports Radio host Jason Whitlock linked top NFL reporter Dianna Russini with NFL coaches Mike Vrabel, Mike Tomlin, and Sean McVay. He drew parallels to fast food chains. He referenced an old X post by Steve Levy. Levy praised Samantha Ponder for noticing Russini’s interview success with two NFL coaches during COVID-19 restrictions. Whitlock also recalled a past statement by Dianna Russini. He interpreted it as a confession about her true aspirations.

title: Dianna Russini rumors: Possible Call Her Daddy podcast appearance
content: Rumors suggest Dianna Russini will appear on Alex Cooper’s Call Her Daddy podcast following her resignation from The Athletic due to alleged photos with Mike Vrabel at Sedona luxury hotel. Cris Carter described the experience as horrific and life altering for Dianna Russini. He highlighted the double standard faced by women in the industry. Where Mike Vrabel’s job could be safe, but Dianna Russini had to resign.

title: Candace Owens reacts to leak as Charlie Kirk clip sparks concern
content: Candace Owens faced fresh online backlash after a strange Charlie Kirk show clip and a leaked chat screenshot went viral. A controversial line from the chat sparked strong reactions, but Owens said it was taken out of context and meant as a joke. The debate now centers on authenticity, editing, and whether the full story is being shown.

title: Lexie Brown confronts Klay Thompson, Megan amid breakup threats
content: Lexie Brown says false rumors linking her to Klay Thompson and Megan Thee Stallion’s reported breakup have led to serious threats. She told Fox News Digital she had no involvement and barely knows Thompson. Brown is now asking both to speak up and clear her name, as online backlash has escalated to safety concerns for her and her family.

title: Bhuvneshwar joins elite club, becomes only second Indian to 350 T20 wickets
content: Bhuvneshwar Kumar achieved a significant T20 milestone, becoming the second Indian bowler to take 350 wickets by dismissing Sai Sudharsan. He further claimed Shubman Gill and Jos Buttler, finishing with figures of 3 for 28 against GT. This performance propelled him to the top of the IPL 2026 Purple Cap standings.

title: 'Make Pluto a planet again': Nasa chief Jared Isaacman calls for status restoration
content: Nasa Administrator Jared Isaacman wants Pluto to be a planet again. He voiced this support in a Senate hearing. Nasa researchers are studying Pluto's status. Pluto was a planet until 2006. The International Astronomical Union reclassified it as a dwarf planet. This decision is still debated by scientists and the public. Isaacman believes Pluto should be a planet.

title: 'Alarming!' TMC posts video of 'gross electoral fraud', calls EC 'puppet'
content: The Trinamool Congress has accused the BJP and the Election Commission of "gross electoral fraud" and "murder of democracy" following alleged ballot box tampering. The party shared CCTV footage, claiming it shows unauthorized opening of ballot boxes ahead of the May 4th counting. TMC leaders have begun a sit-in protest, with Mamata Banerjee expected to visit the site.

title: Virat Kohli’s aggressive send-off to Shubman Gill goes viral - Watch
content: Virat Kohli's animated celebration after taking a sharp catch to dismiss Shubman Gill became a highlight of the RCB vs GT match. Kohli's intense reaction, including a ball-throw and a fired-up 'come on' to Gill, underscored the significance of ending the dangerous innings. He later smiled watching the replay, enjoying the moment.

title: From TACO to NACHO: Trump earns new monicker amid Iran impasse
content: Market analysts have shifted from the 'TACO' nickname for President Trump to 'NACHO' amid escalating tensions over the Strait of Hormuz. This new label reflects growing skepticism that the crucial global energy route will reopen soon, despite ongoing diplomatic efforts and trade disputes.

title: Happy Buddha Purnima 2026: Top 50 wishes, messages and quotes
content: Buddha Purnima 2026, falling on Friday, May 1st, offers a much-needed pause from our hectic lives. This sacred day commemorates the birth, enlightenment, and passing of Gautam Buddha. The article provides 60 thoughtful wishes, messages, and quotes to share, encouraging peace, kindness, and mindful reflection during the long weekend.

title: IPL 2026: Shubman Gill scripts history, becomes first player to...
content: Shubman Gill made history for Gujarat Titans. He became the first player to hit 100 sixes for the team. This happened during their IPL 2026 match against Royal Challengers Bengaluru. Earlier, Gujarat Titans bowlers restricted Bengaluru to 155. Gill then led the charge with a rapid 35 off 10 balls. He smashed 24 runs in one over against Josh Hazlewood.

title: Trump to get Nobel Peace Prize in 2026? Panel shortlists 287 names
content: Over 287 candidates, including Donald Trump, are vying for the 2026 Nobel Peace Prize.  The Norwegian Nobel Committee Secretary noted significant renewal among nominees, emphasizing the prize's enduring relevance amidst global conflicts.  While nominations are confidential, leaders from Cambodia, Israel, and Pakistan have reportedly put Trump forward, though this doesn't guarantee an endorsement.

title: IPL: Virat Kohli left fuming as Jason Holder catch controversy rocks RCB camp
content: A spectacular diving catch by Jason Holder to dismiss Rajat Patidar sparked controversy, with Royal Challengers Bengaluru players and coach Andy Flower questioning the umpire's decision. Despite RCB's protests that the ball may have touched the ground, the umpires upheld the dismissal. This crucial wicket shifted momentum in the match, even as the debate over the catch's legitimacy continued.

title: NHL rumors: Devils eye Filip Gustavsson after $34M Wild deal
content: The New Jersey Devils are also said to be scouting Minnesota Wild goalie Filip Gustavsson following a series of NHL playoff rulings that have cast doubt on his future. Gustavsson has recently renewed a $34 million contract, but his poor performances on the field during the postseason have added more fuel to the NHL trade rumors. As Jacob Markstrom grows older, and long-term issues in goalie Peterson persist, the Devils might consider Gustavsson to be a real offseason option.

title: Mike Vrabel reunites with wife amid Russini distance rumors
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and his wife, Jennifer Vrabel, have tried to stay out of the spotlight but were recently spotted at the Salt Lake City International airport separately on two consecutive days amid all the drama. While Dianna Russini and her husband have tried to disappear from the public eye, it seems not a lot has changed between Mike Vrabel and his wife.

title: 4 drown after cruise boat capsizes in MP's Bargi river; 12 missing, rescue ops on
content: Tragedy struck in Jabalpur, Madhya Pradesh, as a cruise boat capsized in Bargi Dam, resulting in four fatalities. Officials confirmed that while 15 individuals have been rescued, search operations continue for 12 still missing. Rescue teams are actively working to locate all passengers following the devastating incident.

title: ‘No place in Gulf, except...: Iran’s Khamenei slams ‘Great Satan’ US, rejects nuclear curbs
content: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei declared Iran's nuclear and missile capabilities non-negotiable, calling the US the "Great Satan" and asserting that foreigners have no place in the Persian Gulf. He emphasized that Iran's technological and military assets are national treasures to be protected by its population.

title: 4, 4, 4, 4, 4! Virat Kohli joins elite RCB club after tearing apart Rabada | Watch
content: Virat Kohli achieved a special milestone in IPL 2026, smashing five consecutive fours off Kagiso Rabada in a single over. This rare feat places him alongside Chris Gayle and Shane Watson in Royal Challengers Bengaluru history. Despite Rabada striking back later, Kohli's explosive start set a strong tone for RCB.

title: Hema Malini gets emotional remembering Dharmendra’s inspiration
content: Hema Malini became emotional while speaking about her late husband, legendary actor Dharmendra, highlighting his passion for cinema and his connection with the audience. She expressed how much she misses him and reflected on his immense contribution to the film industry, for which he was posthumously honored with the Padma Vibhushan.

title: From Honey Singh to Badshah and Nora Fatehi: Are ‘objectionable’ songs influencing youth behaviour?
content: Authorities across India are taking action against songs and content deemed vulgar and glorifying crime, leading to takedowns and FIRs. This crackdown, involving police, courts, and regulatory bodies, sparks debate on balancing creative freedom with social responsibility, particularly concerning youth influence.

title: IPL 2026: MCC defends ‘obstructing the field’ call on Angkrish Raghuvanshi
content: The Marylebone Cricket Club has defended the controversial 'obstructing the field' dismissal of KKR's Angkrish Raghuvanshi. They clarified that changing direction to run on the pitch, as Raghuvanshi did, constitutes a wilful act under the laws. The MCC emphasised that the batter's intent and actions, not whether they would have been out anyway, are key to such decisions.

title: 'They call Trump Nazi, but still attend': Erika Kirk slams scribes for 'ultimate hypocrisy'
content: Erika Kirk, CEO of Turning Point USA, criticized journalists for labeling President Trump with extreme terms while attending the White House Correspondents' Dinner. She attended to confront media over coverage of her and her late husband, Charlie Kirk, who was murdered in 2025. Kirk linked the attempted White House shooting to a climate of dehumanization fueled by media narratives.

title: Rohit, Kohli’s ‘flow does get disrupted’ as one-format players: Dhawan
content: Shikhar Dhawan believes Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli will adapt to reduced ODI frequency ahead of the 2027 World Cup due to their experience and professionalism. He noted that while less match exposure can disrupt rhythm, these seasoned players know how to manage it. Dhawan also highlighted that upcoming scheduling will provide sufficient ODI games for them to find form.

title: ‘Is he AI? Has Musk created him?’: Buttler’s hilarious Sooryavanshi remark goes viral
content: Cricket sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continues to amaze, with England cricketer Jos Buttler jokingly questioning if the 15-year-old prodigy is 'AI'. Sooryavanshi's witty 'God-gifted' reply to 'AI chip' theories has won hearts.  Buttler lauded his fearless six-hitting against top bowlers, while Sooryavanshi rewrites IPL history, becoming the fastest to 400 runs.

title: Abhijeet on fallout with SRK: ‘His ego, my self-respect’
content: Playback singer Abhijeet Bhattacharya revisited his fallout with Shah Rukh Khan, citing the actor's ego and his own self-respect as the core reasons. He expressed hurt over the lack of an apology, feeling sidelined and denied credit, particularly after feeling his voice was used without proper acknowledgment.

title: 'TMC will get over 226 seats': Mamata rejects exit polls giving BJP edge
content: West Bengal chief minister Mamata Banerjee dismissed exit poll predictions, asserting the Trinamool Congress will secure a decisive victory.  While most surveys indicated a close contest with a slight edge for the BJP, one poll projected a strong win for the TMC.  Both parties expressed confidence in their respective wins.

title: Chhattisgarh Assembly passes resolution for one-third quota for women in legislative bodies
content: Chief Minister Vishnu Deo Sai tabled the resolution and said that for the dignity, overall development and empowerment of women, one-third reservation for them in the Lok Sabha and all Legislative Assemblies should be enforced immediately following the delimitation exercise

title: Counselling-based transfers for sub-registrars in Karnataka to be made permanent exercise
content: Revenue Minister Krishna Byre Gowda has said Karnataka will make its counselling-based transfer system for sub-registrars a permanent, annual exercise, replacing a process that had long been viewed with suspicion

title: Data parks of combined 1,000 MW capacity to be set up in Bengaluru, Mysuru, Mangaluru
content: A preliminary meeting regarding the setting of data parks was held by Karnataka Ministers K.J. George, M.B. Patil, and Priyank Kharge

title: Tamil Nadu records highest marine fish landings in 2025
content: The State landed an estimated 6.85 lakh tonnes of fish, a slight increase from 2024 when it was 6.79 lakh tonnes, despite facing two cyclones; it surpassed Gujarat that landed 6.43 lakh tonnes

title: SEZs must diversify beyond U.S. market and IT services: CEA
content: ‘Half of SEZ exports today flow to the U.S. and the Netherlands. In a fragmenting world, that is concentration risk,’ says Anantha Nageswaran

title: Mizoram declared ‘insurgency-free’ as last militant group lays down arms
content: A total of 43 cadres of the group, including Mr. Sanate, surrendered their weapons to the government during a “homecoming and arms laying ceremony” at Sesawng near Aizawl during the day

title: Tamil Nadu records 99.9% pass in Class 10 ICSE, Class 12 ISC exams
content: A total of 3,305 students from 88 schools across Tamil Nadu appeared for the Class 12 exams. While girls achieved a pass percentage of 99.93%, 99.89% of boys passed

title: HC grants time to nine convicts in Sattankulam custodial death case to file their appeals
content: The nine policemen were sentenced to death by a trial court in Madurai for the death of trader P. Jayaraj and his son J. Benicks in Sattankulam

title: Work on Yettinahole will resume within a week, says D.K. Shivakumar
content: Shivakumar said the Centre had directed the State to pay ₹257 crore towards penalties and mitigation measures, which would be done

title: Slithering fear: snakebite incidents trigger panic in Kerala
content: Increased instances of snakebites and resultant deaths have spread panic in Kerala. Sarath Babu George speaks to members of the bereaved families and herpetologists and other experts to understand the magnitude of the situation

title: Zubeen death probe: Key accused Shyamkanu Mahanta’s bail plea rejected
content: Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma had said in the State Assembly in November last year that Garg’s death was a case of “plain and simple murder”

title: ‘Systemic failures’ made tribal man take his sister’s skeletal remains to bank, finds probe in Odisha
content: Revenue Divisional Commissioner, who is heading a State government probe, said there was delay in issuing death certificate to the deceased’s family

title: Karnataka Cabinet approves Integrated Satellite Township project in Bengaluru South district at a cost of ₹18,133 crore
content: It agrees to provide a bank guarantee to a tune of ₹7,500 crore to the project that will entail acquisition of 7,404 acres in nine villages of Ramanagara taluk in Bengaluru South district

title: Odisha Assembly: BJP, BJD and Congress resort to political one-upmanship over women’s reservation
content: Linking the amendment in Women’s Reservation Bill, which was already passed in Parliament in 2023, to the Delimitation Bill was a clandestine act to push through delimitation without discussion of facts, says BJD chief and Leader of Opposition Naveen Patnaik in special Assembly session

title: Legendary singer Runa Laila says music has a healing touch, it knows no borders
content: Runa Laila will receive the Minar-e-Dilli award at the 15th Delhi International Film Festival; she has called for greater cultural collaboration among SAARC countries

title: J&K police file case over ‘dissemination of secessionist video’
content: The police warned the general public to refrain from creating, sharing, or circulating such unlawful content

title: On interim bail, MP Engineer Rashid meets ailing father in Srinagar
content: Awami Ittehad Party spokesperson says Mr. Rashid adhered strictly to the directions and restrictions laid down by the Delhi High Court and did not address the media or interact with anyone other than his immediate family

title: ₹5 crore community-funded force, accounting for 60% of traffic personnel, keeps Hyderabad’s IT corridor moving
content: 200 traffic marshals deployed across Gachibowli, Raidurgam, Madhapur and Narsingi as IT firms fund Cyberabad traffic management

title: BJP, Opposition spar at special session on women’s empowerment in Uttar Pradesh Assembly
content: By opposing Nari Shakti Vandan Bill, Opposition aims to hinder political rights of women, says Yogi Adityanath; SP accuses BJP of misleading people on the issue of women’s reservation

title: India’s first green methanol plant to turn Kutch weed into marine fuel
content: Prosopis juliflora, which has for decades threatened biodiversity in Kutch’s Banni grasslands, will be turned into methanol; the global shipping industry is being obliged to adopt green fuels under International Maritime Organization rules

title: Remove time cap on legal abortion in minor rape cases: Supreme Court
content: Says State and doctors cannot make such decisions for minor rape survivors; CJI calls for penal law amendment to ensure trials in such cases completed within a week, accused’s property transferred to victim

title: Four killed, 12 missing as cruise boat capsizes in Jabalpur
content: According to police, the Madhya Pradesh Tourism department cruise got stuck in reservoir amid bad weather and heavy rains

title: On first Ladakh visit since violence, Amit Shah to join exposition of holy relics of Lord Buddha in Leh
content: The Centre has already announced May 22 as a fresh date to resume Delhi-Ladakh talks on the demands of the Leh Apex Body and Kargil Democratic Alliance, including Statehood and Sixth Schedule inclusion

title: Make learning Kannada mandatory irrespective of board or curriculum followed: KDA report
content: It has been recommended that Kannada should be made compulsory in schools in Karnataka affiliated to any board, in keeping with the Kannada Language Learning Act, 2015.

title: Assembly election 2026 updates: CM Mamata reaches Kolkata's Bhabanipur EVM strong room, alleges tampering of vote machines
content: Trinamool leaders Kunal Ghosh and Shashi Panja staged a sit-in protest at Khudiram Anushilan Kendra on Thursday, alleging irregular activities inside the EVM strongroom facilities

title: Nearly 5,000 families in Chellanam brace for vagaries of rough sea this monsoon
content: Many families have returned after repairing houses damaged by sea incursions. However, the second-phase construction of the tetrapod seawall, launched with much fanfare ahead of the Assembly elections, has made little progress

title: Odissi elegance casts a spell at Koodalmanikyam festival
content: Madhulita Mohapatra and Nrityantar Ensemble weave devotion and artistry into a captivating performance

title: Empty LNG ship from India heads into Strait of Hormuz for loading in UAE’s Das Island
content: Umm Al Ashtan is among the first instance since the war started that an empty ship in ballast condition is sailing upstream of the Strait of Hormuz

title: Railways Minister flags off first Jammu-Srinagar direct train service on ‘historic day’
content: Terming it ‘India’s first experience with railway operations at such high altitudes,’ Ashwini Vaishnaw calls it a ‘gamechanger for the region’s economy’

title: Devotees throng streets of Madurai to catch a glimpse of Lord Kallazhagar
content: Devotees gathered for ‘Ethir Sevai’ at various points of the city to welcome the deity who left His abode at Alagarkoil on Wednesday evening, as part of the annual Chithirai festival

title: Chithirai festival is not merely a temple event, but a civilisational continuum: Madurai Bench
content: The Madras High Court has issued a set of directions for the authorities to prevent miscreants from throwing footwear on devotees during Lord Kallazhagar’s ceremonial journey culminating in the deity’s entry into the Vaigai

title: Watch: Supreme Court to Centre: Amend law on pregnancy termination | Above the Fold | 30.04.2026
content: From the Supreme Court’s observations on minor pregnancy termination laws, Kargil’s concerns over Ladakh’s new districts to Bengaluru rains causing widespread damage and global tensions around the Iran war. Here are the top stories of the day.

title: Delhi HC freezes Sunjay Kapur assets amid inheritance dispute
content: Court passed interim order in response to a plea by Kapur’s children with his second wife Karisma Kapoor; they are challenging a purported will leaving the whole estate to Kapur’s third wife Priya Kapur

title: Trinamool stages sit-in outside EVM strongroom in Kolkata alleging irregularities
content: Trinamool sources said that CM Mamata Banerjee was also heading to the centre to take stock of the situation.

title: UP Man Who Murdered 3 In Gym Over Birthday Cake Killed In Encounter
content: Saini was taken to a hospital and later referred to a district facility, where doctors declared him dead.

title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Trump Floats New Plan Aimed At Creating Maritime Alliance To Reopen Strait Of Hormuz
content: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran's President Masoud Pezeshkian told Pakistani PM Shehbaz Sharif during a phone call Saturday night that the US "should first remove operational obstacles, including the blockade"

title: Delivery Agent, New To Delhi, Dies After Fall From Flyover As Car Rams Bike
content: A 25-year-old food delivery agent's first week in the city met a tragic end on Thursday when he fell to his death from the Dabri flyover in west Delhi after a car allegedly hit his scooter, police said.

title: Trinamool Holds Sit-In, Alleges Ballot Boxes Opened. "Pure Rumours," Says BJP
content: The protest took place outside a strong room at Netaji Indoor Stadium in central Kolkata, where EVMs for several Assembly constituencies in north Kolkata are kept.

title: Gurugram Man Racks Up Rs 1 Lakh In Challans, More Than Cost Of His Bike
content: According to the police, the accused allegedly violated multiple provisions of the Motor Vehicles Act, with offences recorded both manually and through CCTV surveillance cameras.

title: US Blockade Of Ports An "Extension Of Military Operations": Iran President
content: Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian said that a US naval blockade of his country's ports was effectively an "extension of military operations" by Washington, in spite of an ongoing ceasefire between the two sides.

title: 'Central Staff Can Be Counting Supervisors': Court Rejects Trinamool Appeal
content: The Calcutta High Court refused to interfere with the counting process, emphasising that judicial intervention during an ongoing election is limited

title: UP Judge Duped Of Rs 23.99 Lakh Pretext Of Arranging Haj Pilgrimage: Cops
content: Two suspected cyber fraudsters have been arrested for allegedly duping a judge here of Rs 23.99 lakh on the pretext of arranging a Haj pilgrimage, police said on Thursday.

title: "Only Way Out": Rape Survivor Sets Self Afire Near Gurugram Top Cop's Office
content: A rape survivor's desperate cry for justice spilled onto the streets outside the Gurugram Police Commissioner's office on Thursday, when she allegedly attempted to end her life by setting herself on fire, claiming months of police inaction in her case.

title: US To Execute Man, Who Spent Nearly 50 Years On Death Row, For Killing Minor
content: A Florida man who has spent nearly 50 years on Death Row is to be executed by lethal injection on Thursday for the murder of a 13-year-old girl.

title: "Reserves": Pak Minister Contrasts Country's Oil Shock With India's Stability
content: Pakistan's Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik pointed at how India is relatively stable compared to his country because of its strategic oil reserves and adequate forex reserves, which absorbed the impact of the oil disruption due to the blockade i

title: Top Court Orders Test Of Audio 'Linking' Biren Singh To Manipur Violence
content: More than 260 people were killed and thousands displaced since the ethnic violence broke out between the Imphal valley-based Meitei and neighbouring hills-based Kuki communities in May 2023.

title: 700 Paramilitary Companies Deployed To Prevent Bengal Post-Poll Violence
content: Officials at the Election Commission headquarters will conduct real-time monitoring of the counting exercise through control rooms.

title: Man Gets Death Sentence For Killing 4 Children At Nursery School In Uganda
content: A Ugandan court sentenced a man to death on Thursday for killing four young children this month at a nursery school, in an attack that sparked public outrage and concern for pupils' safety in the East African country.

title: BJP Sweep In Bengal, Trinamool A Distant Second, Predicts Today's Chanakya Exit Poll
content: The BJP, Today's Chanakya predicted, could get 192 seats (plus/minus 11 seats) and the Trinamool Congress 100 seats (plus/minus 11 seats). The rest could get two seats (plus/minus two seats).

title: Man, 31, Accused In Trump Assassination Attempt Agrees To Remain In Custody
content: The man accused of attempting to assassinate President Donald Trump at a black-tie press gala in Washington agreed on Thursday to remain in custody while his case moves forward.

title: 'Preparing ground for their defeat': BJP takes jab at TMC over vote rigging claims
content: the Bharatiya Janata party hit out at the All India Trinamool Comgress . BJP leader tapas Roy says the ruling party leaders are spreading rumours . the BJP is preparing ground for their defeat in the assembly polls - he says .

title: Moving beyond LPG: Which alternatives can power Indian kitchens next?
content: Translating this potential into large-scale adoption requires time, investment, and resolution of structural challenges. These include consistent feedstock aggregation, efficient processing systems, and last-mile logistics. In comparison, LPG benefits from a well-established nationwide distribution network and has played a pivotal role in expanding clean cooking access. In the current context, biogas and CBG are best positioned as complementary solutions rather than immediate substitutes for LPG.
